hero.title: Aizen is a macOS workspace for parallel development.
hero.subtitle: Bring order to your projects, environments, and day-to-day work.
hero.description: Give each project or branch its own environment, with its own terminal, files, browser, agents, and state.
hero.requirements: macOS 13.5+ on Apple Silicon or Intel. Signed and notarized builds available.

workspaces.title: Workspace Management
workspaces.desc: Group active projects into workspaces, then move between environments without losing your place.

worktrees.title: Project Environments
worktrees.desc: Give each project or branch its own environment. Under the hood, Aizen can use worktrees, folders, or full copies.

agents.title: Agentic-First
agents.desc: Run Claude, Codex, OpenCode, Gemini, and other ACP agents where the work happens. Add MCP servers from the built-in marketplace.

terminal.title: Persistent Terminal
terminal.desc: GPU-accelerated terminal powered by libghostty with split panes, presets, themes, and optional tmux persistence.

git.title: Git, Review, and CI
git.desc: Stage, commit, diff, review pull requests, and keep GitHub Actions or GitLab CI runs within reach.

voice.title: Environment Context
voice.desc: Use voice input, file attachments, and per-environment chat history to keep agent context tied to the work.

fileBrowser.title: File Browser
fileBrowser.desc: Browse, search, and inspect files with syntax highlighting, multi-tab editing, and inline diff support.

webBrowser.title: Web Browser
webBrowser.desc: Use the built-in browser for docs, previews, auth flows, and local apps. Tabs stay with each worktree session.

step1.title: Set up projects, not plumbing
step1.desc: Bring related projects into one workspace, then create environments for the branches or tasks that are active right now.

step2.title: Keep every environment ready
step2.desc: Each environment keeps its own terminal panes, file tabs, browser tabs, chat history, and review context.

step3.title: Let agents work with you
step3.desc: Run agents, watch workflow runs, review diffs, or open the same project from the CLI without rebuilding your setup.

q1.question: What problem does Aizen solve?
q1.answer: Developers working across multiple projects or branches usually juggle terminals, editor windows, docs, CI dashboards, and agent chats. Aizen keeps that stack attached to each environment so the context moves with the work.

q2.question: What are Git worktrees?
q2.answer: Git worktrees let you check out multiple branches simultaneously in separate directories. Unlike stashing or switching branches, each worktree is independent. You can build one branch while editing another.

q3.question: How is this different from VS Code or other editors?
q3.answer: Editors focus on one project at a time. Aizen is built around parallel environments: persistent terminal panes, built-in browser tabs, agent sessions, diffs, and workflow visibility. It's a workspace for parallel development, not just a text editor.

q4.question: Which agents are supported?
q4.answer: Aizen is agentic-first and ACP-first. You can add registry-backed agents such as Claude, Codex, OpenCode, Gemini, and other ACP-compatible tools, or configure your own custom command. MCP servers can be added per agent from inside the app.

q5.question: Is Aizen free?
q5.answer: Yes. The app is free to download and open source under the GPL-3.0 license. The current core experience — workspaces, project environments, agents, terminal, file browser, browser, CLI, and workflow views — is available without paying.

q6.question: What's Aizen Pro?
q6.answer: Aizen Pro funds ongoing development. Pro subscribers get priority support and will receive paid extras as they ship, while the free app remains usable on its own. Available as monthly ($5.99/mo), yearly ($59/yr), or lifetime ($179).

q7.question: Can I use one license on multiple Macs?
q7.answer: Yes. Your Pro license can be activated on multiple Macs you own. Each device is registered to your license, and you can manage devices from the billing portal.

q8.question: What's the refund policy?
q8.answer: We offer a 30-day money-back guarantee. If you're not satisfied, email dev@aizen.win within 30 days for a full refund, no questions asked.

q9.question: What are the requirements?
q9.answer: macOS 13.5 or later. Apple Silicon and Intel Macs are both supported.
